title: Author example/README.md with the add-a-new-widget playbook
description: Write example/README.md so a future contributor adding a new widget to turbo_widgets knows exactly the four things they need to do to make the new widget show up in the shop showcase. The README is the source of truth for keeping the showcase in lockstep with the widget library — it does not duplicate package documentation.

context.why_this_exists: The spec calls out that the showcase must stay in sync with the widget library as new widgets are added. Without a written playbook, future contributors will add widgets and forget to wire them into the showcase, rotting the example.
context.what_the_readme_is_not: Not a full architectural essay. Not a tutorial on dev-conventions. Not a walkthrough of turbo_widgets internals. Keep it crisp and actionable.
context.audience: A developer who already knows Flutter and the turbo_* package family but has never worked on this specific example.

goals: example/README.md exists and documents the exact four steps to add a new product page when a new widget is added to turbo_widgets.
The steps match what task 19's final wiring actually requires — not a hypothetical process.
The README includes a short orientation paragraph on the shop metaphor and one diagram-free paragraph on the folder layout so a new contributor can navigate the tree quickly.
The README is discoverable from the repo root via the existing package README chain.
non_goals: Documenting individual widgets — they have their own dartdoc.
Copying the entire dev-conventions skill into the README.
acceptance_criteria: example/README.md contains a clear numbered list of the four steps: (1) add ShowcaseRoute enum value, (2) add ShowcaseCatalogService entry, (3) create the product detail view + view model folder, (4) register the route in ShowcaseRouter.
Each step names the exact file path that must change.
A new contributor reading the README alone can add a fictional widget to the showcase in under 15 minutes without reading any other file.
The file renders cleanly as GitHub-flavoured markdown.
constraints: Markdown only. No screenshots, no diagrams, no binary assets.
Use the exact filenames produced by phases 2–5, verified by looking at the files on disk.
Do not describe any step that involves editing a file that was not part of the realised implementation.
tools_to_use: RepoPrompt MCP for confirming file paths match reality.
Bash for `ls` and quick sanity checks on the referenced files.

testing_and_verification.unit_tests: Not applicable — this task produces documentation, not code.
verification_gate: Every file path named in the README exists on disk (grep-verifiable).
A second agent reading only the README should be able to enumerate all four steps without confusion — verify by re-reading it cold after a break.

quality_checklist.maintainability: The README is the single source of truth for the add-a-widget flow. No duplication in CLAUDE.md or the package README.
quality_checklist.single_responsibility: This README documents the showcase-sync flow. Nothing else.

workflow_instructions: Before writing, list the files produced by phases 2–5 with `ls` and pin down exact filenames.
After this task, there are no more tasks to enrich — but scan archive/dev and archive/ui once more in case a `follow_up_notes` entry was missed during earlier archives.
